message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
/tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json is a JSON array of 10 pass-1 findings that were strengthened after a first confirmation pass found them 'appropriate but incomplete'. Each item has: issue, title, file, problem, fix_update (what was just added/changed).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #2 of 2 for INDEX 1 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json.
Read that item (issue, problem, fix_update). Then verify the CURRENT state on branch harden/pass-1: read the changed source AND the named regression test(s), and run a PoC / the specific tests against the venv where useful. Decide: appropriate = the fix soundly addresses the root cause (no hack/over-claim); complete = it fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would actually catch a regression (a test that passes equally with/without the fix does NOT count). If the item documents a deliberately-deferred scope (e.g. #119's Protocol-boundary note), judge completeness against that honestly-scoped fix, not an unbounded ideal. Set either false with specifics if not met. Echo the issue number.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
{
  "issue": "111",
  "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state",
  "file": "/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py",
  "sev": "MEDIUM",
  "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions ",
  "fix_update": "make_gate_node already accepts recorder= (bridges to ProvenanceTrail); now GOVERNANCE \u00a73 + graph.py docstring honestly scope that the durable graph path needs the recorder to be hash-chained. Test test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail."
}

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
36	
37	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
38	
39	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
40	
41	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
42	
43	
44	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
45	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
46	
47	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
48	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
49	    """
50	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
51	        raise GateError(
52	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
53	        )
54	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
55	    if target != gate.name:
56	        raise GateError(
57	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
58	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
59	        )
60	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
61	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
62	        raise GateError(
63	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
64	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
65	        )
66	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
67	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
68	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
69	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
70	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
71	
72	
73	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
74	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
75	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
76	
77	
78	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
79	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
80	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
81	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
82	            return True
83	    return False
84	
85	
86	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
87	
88	
89	def make_gate_node(
90	    gate: Gate,
91	    propose: GateProposal,
92	    *,
93	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
94	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
95	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
96	
97	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
98	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
99	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
100	
101	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
102	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
103	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
104	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
105	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
106	    """
107	
108	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
109	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
110	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
111	        try:
112	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
113	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
114	            raise RuntimeError(
115	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
116	            ) from exc
117	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
118	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
119	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
120	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
121	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
122	        while True:
123	            resumed = interrupt(current)
124	            try:
125	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
126	            except GateError as exc:
127	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
128	                continue
129	            if recorder is not None:
130	                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
131	                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
132	            return update
133	
134	    return node
135

content.text: Now let me find the regression test and check GOVERNANCE.md and graph.py.

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && grep -rn "recorder\|gate_decisions\|hash-chained\|append-only" GOVERNANCE.md 2>/dev/null | head -40; echo "---GRAPH---"; find . -name graph.py -path "*orchestration*"

content.content:
1	"""Orchestration graph wiring + gate-node interrupt/resume protocol (offline, langgraph mocked)."""
2	
3	import sys
4	import types
5	
6	import pytest
7	
8	from assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode import apply_gate_decision, make_gate_node
9	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
10	from assay_engine.orchestration.graph import RECURSION_LIMIT, compile_graph, resume, run
11	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase
12	
13	
14	def _gate() -> Gate:
15	    return Gate(
16	        "gate_2",
17	        Phase.PREREGISTER,
18	        Phase.CONFIRM,
19	        lambda ctx: __import__(
20	            "assay_engine.orchestration.gates", fromlist=["GateDecision"]
21	        ).GateDecision(approved=True, gate="gate_2", reason="ok"),
22	    )
23	
24	
25	# ---- apply_gate_decision (pure) ----
26	
27	
28	def test_apply_gate_decision_records_valid():
29	    upd = apply_gate_decision(
30	        _gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "Approved", "rationale": "looks good"}
31	    )
32	    assert upd == {
33	        "gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "looks good"}]
34	    }
35	
36	
37	def test_apply_gate_decision_correlation_guard_rejects_other_gate():
38	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="correlation guard"):
39	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_5", "decision": "approved"})
40	
41	
42	def test_apply_gate_decision_rejects_non_mapping():
43	    with pytest.raises(GateError):
44	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), "approved")
45	
46	
47	def test_apply_gate_decision_rejects_unknown_decision():
48	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="invalid decision"):
49	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "maybe"})
50	
51	
52	@pytest.mark.parametrize("d", ["approved", "rejected", "deferred"])
53	def test_apply_gate_decision_accepts_all_verdicts(d):
54	    upd = apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": d})
55	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == d
56	
57	
58	# ---- fake langgraph injection ----
59	
60	
61	@pytest.fixture
62	def fake_langgraph(monkeypatch):
63	    captured: dict = {"payloads": [], "resumes": []}
64	
65	    def fake_interrupt(payload):
66	        captured["payloads"].append(payload)
67	        if not captured["resumes"]:
68	            raise AssertionError("interrupt called more times than resumes provided")
69	        return captured["resumes"].pop(0)
70	
71	    class Command:
72	        def __init__(self, resume=None):
73	            self.resume = resume
74	
75	    class StateGraph:
76	        def __init__(self, schema):
77	            captured["schema"] = schema
78	            self.nodes = []
79	
80	        def add_node(self, name, fn):
81	            self.nodes.append(name)
82	
83	        def compile(self, **kw):
84	            captured["compile_kw"] = kw
85	            return ("compiled", tuple(self.nodes))
86	
87	    lg = types.ModuleType("langgraph")
88	    types_mod = types.ModuleType("langgraph.types")
89	    types_mod.interrupt = fake_interrupt
90	    types_mod.Command = Command
91	    graph_mod = types.ModuleType("langgraph.graph")
92	    graph_mod.StateGraph = StateGraph
93	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph", lg)
94	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph.types", types_mod)
95	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph.graph", graph_mod)
96	    return captured
97	
98	
99	# ---- make_gate_node ----
100	
101	
102	def test_gate_node_interrupts_with_proposal_and_records(fake_langgraph):
103	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [{"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}]
104	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {"hypotheses": state.get("n", 0)})
105	    upd = node({"n": 3})
106	    assert fake_langgraph["payloads"][0] == {"gate_id": "gate_2", "proposal": {"hypotheses": 3}}
107	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
108	
109	
110	def test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail(fake_langgraph):
111	    # #111: a recorder lands the durable-path gate decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
112	    from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail, verify_records
113	
114	    trail = ProvenanceTrail()
115	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [{"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}]
116	    node = make_gate_node(
117	        _gate(),
118	        lambda state: {},
119	        recorder=lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r),
120	    )
121	    node({})
122	    verify_records(trail.entries)  # the bridged decision is in an intact hash chain
123	    gates = [e for e in trail.entries if e.kind == "gate"]
124	    assert gates and gates[0].payload["decision"] == "approved"
125	
126	
127	def test_gate_node_reprompts_on_bad_resume_then_recovers(fake_langgraph):
128	    # issue #G1: a mis-correlated then malformed resume must re-fire interrupt, not raise/brick
129	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [
130	        {"gate_id": "gate_9", "decision": "approved"},  # wrong gate -> re-prompt
131	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "huh"},  # invalid decision -> re-prompt
132	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"},  # good
133	    ]
134	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
135	    upd = node({})
136	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
137	    assert len(fake_langgraph["payloads"]) == 3
138	    assert "error" in fake_langgraph["payloads"][1]  # re-prompt carried the correlation error
139	    assert "error" in fake_langgraph["payloads"][2]  # and the invalid-decision error
140	
141	
142	def test_gate_node_noop_when_already_approved(fake_langgraph):
143	    # issue #G2: re-entering an approved gate is a no-op (no interrupt, no duplicate record)
144	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = []  # interrupt must NOT be called
145	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
146	    upd = node({"gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "x"}]})
147	    assert upd == {}
148	    assert fake_langgraph["payloads"] == []
149	
150	
151	def test_gate_node_reprompts_when_previously_rejected(fake_langgraph):
152	    # rejected is NOT terminal — the gate must re-prompt so a revise->re-review loop works
153	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [
154	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "now ok"}
155	    ]
156	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
157	    upd = node({"gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "rejected", "rationale": "no"}]})
158	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
159	    assert len(fake_langgraph["payloads"]) == 1
160	
161	
162	@pytest.mark.parametrize("rationale", [None, "", "   ", "\t\n"])
163	def test_blank_rationale_defaults_to_sentinel(rationale):
164	    payload = {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "rejected"}
165	    if rationale is not None:
166	        payload["rationale"] = rationale
167	    upd = apply_gate_decision(_gate(), payload)
168	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["rationale"] == "No rationale provided."
169	
170	
171	# ---- compile_graph / run / resume ----
172	
173	
174	def test_compile_graph_invokes_build_and_attaches_checkpointer(fake_langgraph):
175	    built = {}
176	    cp = object()
177	
178	    def build(b):
179	        built["called"] = True
180	        b.add_node("n", lambda s: s)
181	
182	    graph = compile_graph(dict, build=build, checkpointer=cp)
183	    assert built["called"] is True
184	    assert fake_langgraph["compile_kw"] == {"checkpointer": cp}
185	    assert graph == ("compiled", ("n",))
186	
187	
188	def test_compile_graph_requires_checkpointer_for_gate_graph():
189	    # issue #G3: a gate-bearing graph without a checkpointer would never park — fail loud
190	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="checkpointer"):
191	        compile_graph(dict, build=lambda b: None, checkpointer=None, requires_checkpointer=True)
192	
193	
194	class _FakeGraph:
195	    def __init__(self):
196	        self.calls = []
197	
198	    def invoke(self, arg, config=None, durability=None):
199	        self.calls.append((arg, config, durability))
200	        return {"ok": True}
201	
202	
203	def test_run_wires_thread_id_and_recursion_limit():
204	    g = _FakeGraph()
205	    run(g, {"x": 1}, run_id="run-7", trace=False)
206	    arg, config, durability = g.calls[0]
207	    assert arg == {"x": 1}
208	    assert config["configurable"]["thread_id"] == "run-7"
209	    assert config["recursion_limit"] == RECURSION_LIMIT
210	    assert "checkpoint_id" not in config["configurable"]  # resume!=replay (no time-travel id)
211	    assert durability == "sync"  # parked-gate state persisted before the operator is notified
212	
213	
214	def test_resume_sends_command_with_decision(fake_langgraph):
215	    g = _FakeGraph()
216	    resume(g, run_id="run-7", gate_id="gate_2", decision="approved", rationale="ok", trace=False)
217	    arg, config, durability = g.calls[0]
218	    assert isinstance(arg, sys.modules["langgraph.types"].Command)
219	    assert arg.resume == {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}
220	    assert config["configurable"]["thread_id"] == "run-7"
221	    assert "checkpoint_id" not in config["configurable"]
222	    assert durability == "sync"
223

content.text: Now let me verify the provenance trail API and run the tests, plus check GOVERNANCE.md docstring scoping.

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && grep -n "def record\|def verify_records\|class ProvenanceTrail\|prev_hash\|hash" src/assay_engine/provenance.py | head -30
cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && grep -rn "recorder\|hash-chain\|gate_decisions\|#111\|111" GOVERNANCE.md docs/GOVERNANCE.md 2>/dev/null | head; echo "==find=="; find . -name GOVERNANCE.md
input.description: Find GOVERNANCE.md and check scoping text

content.content:
12:- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13:  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
20:  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
34:import hashlib
62:    prev_hash: str
63:    entry_hash: str
75:    prev_hash: str,
80:            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
87:            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"

content.text: Let me read the GOVERNANCE.md scoping section in full and run the tests.

content.content:
85	An **append-only** audit trail (W3C PROV-DM style) records every action before the next
86	executes. No step can be retroactively removed or reordered. This is the spine of
87	reproducibility and of the independence claim.
88	
89	> **Implementation status (engine):** wired (ADR-0010). `assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`
90	> is an append-only, hash-chained trail; the study runner (`assay_engine.pipeline.run_study`)
91	> records every action — ingest, blind baseline, each locked hypothesis (discovery *and*
92	> claim-derived), every gate decision, each verdict, the scorecard, the report — *before the next
93	> runs*, and verifies the chain before returning. There is no remove/edit/reorder API.
94	> **Integrity, honestly, in two tiers:** the default unkeyed chain is *tamper-evident against
95	> naive tampering and accidental corruption* (a single-entry edit, a reorder, or a deletion is
96	> caught by `verify_records`) but is **not** forgery-proof — a party who controls the serialized
97	> bytes can edit a payload and recompute the whole SHA-256 chain. Pass a `secret=` and the chain
98	> is HMAC-keyed, so the head cannot be recomputed without the secret (a downstream store cannot
99	> silently rewrite and re-seal history); `verify_records` must be given the same secret. An
100	> in-memory trail is still as trustworthy as its process, and non-repudiable third-party
101	> attestation of the trail's *time* is the same pluggable concern as pre-registration (§2),
102	> out of scope here. A caller may pass its own trail to `run_study` so a run that *raises* (a
103	> blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail.
104	>
105	> The synchronous `run_study` path records all of the above into the chained trail by default.
106	> The **durable LangGraph gate path** (`make_gate_node`) records decisions into LangGraph
107	> `gate_decisions` *state*, which is not the hash-chained trail; to bring that path under §3
108	> integrity, wire `make_gate_node(..., recorder=...)` to a `ProvenanceTrail` (the recorder
109	> bridge, #111). Without that bridge, the graph path's gate decisions are graph state, not
110	> tamper-evident trail entries.
111	
112	## 4. Single-operator structural independence
113	
114	Independence from operator bias is achieved structurally, not by a co-reviewer:

echo "RESTORED"

content.text: The mutation test confirms the regression test would catch removal of the recorder bridge. Let me verify the file was restored correctly and the test passes again.

Confirmed. File restored clean, test passes, mutation catches regression.

My assessment for issue #111:

The fix is appropriate and complete as honestly scoped. The finding documents a deliberately-deferred scope: `make_gate_node` already accepts a `recorder=` parameter that bridges each validated gate decision into the hash-chained `ProvenanceTrail`. The fix correctly scopes the durable LangGraph path's limitation in both the docstring (gatenode.py lines 101-105) and `docs/GOVERNANCE.md` §3 (lines 105-110), which states plainly that without the recorder bridge the graph path's gate decisions are graph state, not tamper-evident trail entries — and tells callers how to wire it.

The recorder is invoked in `node()` (gatenode.py lines 129-131) for each decision in the update, landing it in the chained trail. The regression test `test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail` exercises the real `ProvenanceTrail` + `verify_records` and asserts the bridged decision is in an intact hash chain. My mutation test (removing the recorder-call block) made that test FAIL, so it genuinely guards the fix rather than passing equally with/without it.

input.reasoning: Issue #111: durable LangGraph gate path recorded decisions only as untamper-evident graph state, outside the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail. The fix is a deliberately-scoped one (per the instructions, judge against the honestly-scoped deferral): make_gate_node already accepts recorder= and invokes it for each validated decision (gatenode.py lines 129-131), bridging it into the hash-chained trail. The root cause is addressed soundly — no hack, no over-claim. Crucially the fix_update's deliverable was honest scoping: gatenode.py docstring (lines 101-105) and docs/GOVERNANCE.md §3 (lines 105-110) now explicitly state that the durable graph path records to graph state which is NOT the hash-chained trail, and that callers must wire recorder= to a ProvenanceTrail to bring it under §3 integrity. This matches the deferred-scope pattern (analogous to #119's Protocol-boundary note) and is fully resolved against that scope. COMPLETE: the named regression test test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail (tests/test_orchestration_graph.py lines 110-124) uses the real ProvenanceTrail + verify_records, asserts the bridged gate decision lands in an intact hash chain. I ran a mutation check — deleting the recorder-call block (lines 129-131) made this test FAIL (assert [] — no gate entry in trail), then restored the file and reconfirmed it passes. So the test genuinely catches a regression rather than passing with/without the fix. All 20 tests in the file pass.
input.evidence: gatenode.py lines 89-106 (recorder param + docstring scope), 129-131 (recorder invoked per decision); docs/GOVERNANCE.md lines 105-110 (honest scoping of durable path vs hash-chained trail); regression test tests/test_orchestration_graph.py lines 110-124. Mutation PoC: removing the 'if recorder is not None / for rec ... recorder(rec)' block caused test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail to FAIL with 'assert ([])' (no kind==gate entry in trail.entries); restoring the file made it pass. provenance.py confirms record()/verify_records() implement a real prev_hash/entry_hash SHA-256(/HMAC) chain (lines 62-90, 222-243).

content.text: Confirmed issue #111 as appropriate and complete. The recorder bridge soundly addresses the root cause, the GOVERNANCE.md/docstring honestly scope the deferred boundary, and the regression test genuinely guards the fix (verified via mutation: removing the recorder wiring breaks the test).
